title: Lesson 266
transcript: We've talked about how asset classification is there to protect assets. The way that we protect assets is to classify them because classification is something that affords assets the level of protection that they require based on their assets but before we can classify assets there is something very important that companies need to be able to do and that is we need to be able to first of all know what we have to classify and protect And so this is where organizations typically fail. But the first step in protecting assets is actually to know what assets you actually have. Because unless you know what you have, how can you protect those assets based on classification systems? And so some sort of asset inventory is what's required by organizations, but that inventory needs to be able to capture all assets as we acquire them or create them or inherit them you know whatever way we get assets we need to know what they are and capture them into that inventory once we know what we have the next step if you think about it logically is to identify who the owners are and that's a very important step and again where most companies fail because the owners are always going to be in the best position to to be able to understand the value of those assets and therefore classify them which starts the entire asset protection life cycle so knowing what we have identifying the owners because the owners will classify those assets based on value is really the process that we need to go through in order to protect assets properly. So here we say data classification, but it should more appropriately say asset classification. But the reason we say data classification is because that's what it says in the exam outline. but anytime you see the word data in any especially this domain number two which is all about asset security data and information is only one example of important valuable assets that any organization would want to protect so if you see the word data or information even on the exam I think what you should do is replace it in your mind with the word asset because we want to protect assets based on value and classification systems is what allows us to do that. So fundamentally, whenever you see data classification, replace it in your mind with asset classification. And here is that process that we talked about, right? Before we can actually protect assets based on value, we need to know what we have. And so we need to be able to identify assets that need to be protected using classification. And of course, once we know what assets we have, the next step is to identify who the owners are, because the owners will start that process. They will classify from the inventory so that we can start protecting those assets based on classification systems. So here's a really good definition of data classification but again you can replace that word data in your mind with the word asset. Asset classification is something that affords assets the level of protection that they require based on their value. And so the important thing to relate here is that the classification systems are there really to allow us to protect assets, not just show the value. Obviously, showing the value is something that's very important, but the whole purpose of why we classify assets is to protect them based on their value and what's important to remember and we'll make this point again later assets as they go through their life cycle may increase and decrease in value. Your asset classification systems need to be able to support that too. An increase in classification or a decrease in classification so that we can always protect assets based on their value. That's what dictates how we protect things. So that's a pretty good definition. Now you should know the answer to this. Who determines the classification level? At least what we start with. Always going to be the owner. But that's where a lot of companies fail. in being able to identify who the actual owners are because they are in the best position to understand what the value of those assets actually are. And here's another problem. I mean, what owner does not want to say that what they own is the most critical, valuable in the organization. Every owner wants to say, oh, I own the most valuable thing to this organization. until you explain to them the accountability that goes along with that in protecting those assets and how much it might cost. And then the owners will take a step back and say, oh, I don't want that much accountability. So maybe I should under classify because that requires less accountability. So now there's a tendency for owners to under classify. And so how do you address this problem? Well, the way that you can address this problem, and most companies do this, is you establish what's called an asset classification board or committee that's going to be made up of key people, from key areas of the organization that can oversee not only the initial classification process that the owner will start with and drive, But oversee the entire classification system so that we can ultimately protect all assets of the organization based on and always framed in the perspective of goals and objectives of the organization itself. Pretty important for us to do that. Now, as a summary, what we provide here is a life cycle or an overall look of how the classification process looks. So we're going to start with that asset inventory, which should capture all of your assets as you buy them. as you inherit them, as you collect them, for example. That starts the process. And of course, once you know what assets you have, the next step is determine and assign ownership. We have to know who the owners are. because they are the ones that are going to classify based on value. They are in the best position to understand the value of those assets. And then from that point, what we do is we protect all assets based on that value. So as those assets go through the entire life cycle, we protect them and whether they increase or decrease in classification it's not going to make a difference because we know how to protect based on the classification levels that those assets may increase or decrease to and then of course at some point We're going to assess and review how this is working and then we repeat the entire cycle itself. So this is a summary of what classification really means and what it looks like. And again, the whole purpose of asset classification is to protect the assets as they go through their life cycle. But it has to start with the owners because they are in the best position to understand what that value is that drives how we protect it.
